messages.subject: [R-gui]  Tinn-R and JGR issues under Vista
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui]  Tinn-R and JGR issues under Vista
messages.body_plain:
Hi all
I'm kinda new to R and everything else, so please be kind :P
I have some issues running Tinn-R 2.2.0.2 and JGR (latest version) on my
laptop (Windows Vista 64bit)
1) As long as I use R 2.8.1 it works just fine
2) If I run Tinn-R, the "Send Line" command works fine, while the "Send
Selection" works but it gives me always the same answer
"source(.trPaths[5])". I know it works 'cos my script generates a .txt file
in the working directory. Nevertheless, in the R Console no display is shown
(even dir() shows nothing). I don't think it's related to the auth policy of
Vista, as the problem didn't disappear after giving free access to the temp
folder and running Tinn-R as Administrator
3) I installed JGR, loadad the packages (as Admin), installed them,
everything went smooth...but when I launch JGR it opens and then closes one
second after. I tried with the "-debug" option but the debug window closes
too, so I'm kinda stuck...
I hope someone can help me :)
See you all guys! Tnx!

Claudio
-- 
View this message in context: http://n2.nabble.com/Tinn-R-and-JGR-issues-under-Vista-tp2668568p2668568.html
Sent from the R Sig Gui mailing list archive at Nabble.com.

messages.subject: [R-gui] rank function problem
messages.body_plain:
Ibtesam,

you are using the wrong mailing list, you should be using R-help, not 
the GUI list.

Anyway, if you execute "print(z,digits=20)", you see that the reason for 
your different ranks is in numerical accuracy of representations of the 
numbers.

Regards,
Ulrike

ibtesam al_sagaf schrieb:
> Dear all
>
>  
>
> I'm Ibtesam. 
>
> I use R program in my studded.
> I have been seeing  problem in rank function. It works as a wrong way from vector to other.
>  
> I run this code many times, in every time I obtained this result;
>  
> 1-     When I enter this data vector manually, I obtain the correct ranking. It gives the same rank for equal values.  
> Example from the following data: rank(abs(0.2,0.2,-0.2))= 4  4 4
>  
>   
>> k=c(-.9,.2,-1.1,.5,-.8,.3,-1,.6,-1.3,-.2,-1.5,.1,-1.2,-.1,-1.4,.2,-.6,.5,-.8,.8)
>> rank(abs(k))
>>     
>  [1] 14.0  4.0 16.0  7.5 12.0  6.0 15.0  9.5 18.0  4.0 20.0  1.5 17.0  1.5 19.0
> [16]  4.0  9.5  7.5 12.0 12.0
> 2-     When I obtain the same data vector from some operations, I obtain a wrong ranking. It gives the different ranks for equal values.  
> Example from the following data: rank(abs(0.2,-0.2,0.2))= 3  4.5  4.5
>  
>
>
>   
>> x=c(2.9,3,2.5,2.6,3.2)
>> y=c(3.8,2.7,4,2.4)
>> z=rep(0,length(x)*length(y))
>>       sizez=1
>>       for(i in 1:length(x))
>>     
> +          for(j in 1:length(y))
> +      {z[sizez]=x[i]-y[j];  sizez=sizez+1;}
>   
>> z
>>     
>  [1] -0.9  0.2 -1.1  0.5 -0.8  0.3 -1.0  0.6 -1.3 -0.2 -1.5  0.1 -1.2 -0.1 -1.4
> [16]  0.2 -0.6  0.5 -0.8  0.8
>   
>>  R=rank(abs(z)) 
>> R
>>     
>  [1] 14.0  3.0 16.0  7.5 11.5  6.0 15.0 10.0 18.0  4.5 20.0  1.5 17.0  1.5 19.0
> [16]  4.5  9.0  7.5 11.5 13.0
>
>  
>
>  
>
> I try many times but I have not known what is the mistake.
> Can you kindly help me to solve this problem?
>  
> Thank you.
> Ibtesam.
>
>
>
>  
>
> _________________________________________________________________
>
>
> 	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
>

messages.subject: [R-gui] tcltk: change the parent of a widget
messages.subject_clean: [R-gui] tcltk: change the parent of a widget
messages.body_plain:
Dear R-SIG-GUI list readers,

If I have two canvas objects (canvas1 and canvas2), both containing 
items and event bindings:

tt <- tktoplevel()
canvas1 <- tkcanvas(tt, ...)
canvas2 <- tkcanvas(tt, ...)
## ... tkcreate(...); tkitembind(...); ...

and I'd like to display either one canvas in the toplevel window tt, or 
display both, canvas1 and canvas2, in two different toplevel windows 
(say, tt and tt1).

Is there a way to pack canvas1 or canvas2 into another toplevel window 
than the tt window (for which it was defined)? It's important that all 
event bindings stay preserved.

Sincerely,

Adrian Waddell
